narrative: Trump's weekend threat of 25% tariffs on European autos and stalled US-Iran peace talks dominate the tape, setting up a defensive open for the Euro. EUR/USD closed Friday at 1.1723; the tariff escalation and a 175bp carry disadvantage likely to weigh on the pair at the Tokyo open. JPY/USD—G8 Bilateral Theme: JPY 1W avg: +1.081% (n=7), USD 1W avg: -0.874% (n=7), G8 spread: 1.955%. This JPY outperformance, fueled by confirmed $34.5bn intervention, would pressure USD/JPY from Friday's 157.03 close if safe-haven demand persists. RBA's Tuesday meeting remains the primary TIER-1 focus for the AUD/USD open.
